external_controls.scope: main branch protection or ruleset requires Rust Quality before merge
external_controls.evidence_note: The available GitHub connector exposes repository, PR, review and workflow data but not branch protection or rulesets. This control is not claimed as verified.

invariants: Canonical platform remains generic multi-consumer and contains no site-analize-by-pl-specific mandatory concept
Pure crates reject adapter vocabulary; a repository-wide canonical-field scanner does not yet exist.
Mixed proxy remains public on port 1080
HTTP including CONNECT remains public on port 3128
Certificate-pinned TLS/TCP reserve on port 443 remains available
WireGuard remains controlled compatibility and rollback until explicit accepted deprecation
Operator CLI and operator/admin APIs remain preserved
Contract states preservation, but a complete route and CLI inventory parity gate is absent.
Dependencies point inward from foundation through composition roots
Foundation, runtime-domain and the first application crate have layer-specific dependency and vocabulary gates; the complete target graph is not present.
Domain crates reject transport, persistence, Android, filesystem, process, environment and generated-protobuf dependencies
Applies to the currently declared pure crates.
Every mutable aggregate has one owning bounded context
No aggregate owner registry or cross-context mutation scanner exists.
Other contexts mutate aggregate state only through typed application ports
Command issuance and successful acknowledgement mutate through typed application ports; registration, heartbeat and probe are still direct handlers.
Transport handlers only authenticate, authorize, validate, call one use case and map a typed result
Command issue, poll and acknowledgement handlers authenticate at the router, call one typed use case and map typed outcomes; registration, heartbeat and probe remain transitional.
SQL and business transitions are absent from HTTP handlers
Responsibilities move only through small independently testable slices and no big-bang rewrite
Process rule is documented but not machine-enforced.
ADR-001 is superseded only with explicit compatibility migration and equal or stronger controls
Canonical mutable state is durable and not only memory, JSON, YAML, SharedPreferences, process tables or queues
Control-plane state is still JSON backed with in-memory maps.
SQLite uses WAL, foreign keys, busy timeout, single-writer discipline, short transactions, backup and integrity checks
Business state, idempotency result, domain events, audit and outbox commit atomically

Command issuance and successful acknowledgement write queue, durable idempotency result and device projection as one fsynced candidate before in-memory publication; domain event, audit and outbox persistence are absent.
JSON migration performs import, parity validation, write switch, diagnostic export, compatibility window and retirement
Statuses, IDs, error codes, proxy protocols, tunnel transports, strategies, principals, permissions and release reasons are typed
Foundation IDs, runtime lifecycle and runtime fingerprints are typed; other proxy-core taxonomies remain raw strings.
Raw strings are accepted only at transport boundaries and converted immediately
Generated protobuf models do not enter domain crates
Canonical protobuf consumer contracts are introduced.
Every external request has protocol version, request ID, correlation ID and deadline; mutations add idempotency
Request lineage, deadlines and exact/conflicting command idempotency are enforced; protocol version and generic consumer/application idempotency scope are absent.
New internal content, config and binary fingerprints default to full BLAKE3-256 through typed shared wrappers
Release and runtime config/binary fingerprint producers use typed field-specific BLAKE3 contracts.
New internal persisted/public digest text is b3-prefixed full lowercase 64-hex and never bare hex
Every deterministic internal digest uses a static versioned non-user-controlled context and semantic changes bump its version
Raw concatenation is forbidden and each input part uses unsigned u64 big-endian length framing
Structured digest inputs use deterministic versioned canonical bytes and never pretty JSON or Debug output
Release raw bytes and host config canonical JSON are enforced; no generic structured codec exists for future contracts.
Legacy SHA-256 is classified before migration; migration defines reads, writes, conflicts, replay, rollback, retention and persisted fixtures
Release and runtime fingerprint migrations are explicit; a generic schema for future digest migrations remains.
Migration recomputes from canonical source bytes and never computes BLAKE3 over a legacy SHA-256 digest
Config uses canonical redacted JSON and binary uses exact running executable bytes.
New writes create only target BLAKE3; any legacy reader is isolated/versioned and unknown algorithm/domain/version fails closed
Field type fixes domain/version; unknown prefixes and malformed b3 values fail closed.
Digest backfill is restart-safe, observable and reversible until acceptance
Restart-safe cleanup, bounded observability and old-reader JSON rollback compatibility are enforced; legacy adapter removal awaits production acceptance.
Device and VM packages write path-sorted typed integrity manifests with exact sizes and immediately verify all files fail closed
Manifest is consistency evidence, not a signature or trust root.
Foundation validates and formats but never generates randomness, reads clocks, resolves secrets or accesses infrastructure
UUID-backed IDs accept canonical UUIDs and do not implicitly convert between unrelated IDs
Consumer, application and actor IDs are 1-64 allowed ASCII bytes and reject whitespace, controls and credential material
Idempotency keys are 1-128 printable non-whitespace ASCII, opaque, case-sensitive and not logged raw
Validation and non-logging of raw command keys are enforced; repository-wide secret logging detection is absent.
Deadline windows are 1 second to 24 hours and foundation never reads system time
Authenticated edge generates missing request identity, inherits missing correlation, returns both IDs and fails malformed or expired values closed
Authentication precedes lineage parsing and invalid idempotency/deadline values are rejected before command creation
Pending TCP stream entries are removed on success, cancellation and timeout
Global and per-device pending stream counts are bounded and registrations expire
Routing never chooses an arbitrary device and binds accepted streams to expected device and session
Stream authority also binds runtime generation and lease generation
Runtime and lease generations enter production.
Device and session binding is already enforced.
Global tunnel token is replaced by per-device credentials
Primary/reserve transport state and bounded failover reason are tracked
Routing requires heartbeat freshness rather than a stored connected flag
Transport failover and recovery produce durable audit evidence and repeated failures trigger quarantine
Bounded logs and durable counters exist; immutable audit and quarantine do not.
Reserve acceptance proves all proxy protocols, UDP block fallback to TLS/TCP 443, heartbeat, mobile IP and return to QUIC
Permanent software fault injection exists; physical proof remains.
Principal types use separate tokens and consumer scopes cannot invoke provisioning or raw commands
Current admin/device routes are separated; canonical consumer principals are future work.
Persisted bearer-token verifiers use keyed BLAKE3 behind a security port with key ID, rotation and constant-time comparison
A persisted bearer-token verifier is introduced.
Human passwords use Argon2id with random salts and upgradable parameters; fast or unsalted password hashes are forbidden
Protocol-mandated HKDF, HMAC, TLS, signature, AEAD, TLS pin and standardized digest algorithms are preserved exactly
Current TLS and external checksum boundaries are preserved; no complete purpose registry exists.
MD5, SHA-1, implementation-defined hashes, security CRC, unkeyed token hashes, raw concatenation and undocumented crypto dependencies are rejected
Direct SHA-256 and untyped runtime fingerprint producers are rejected; the broader prohibited-primitive inventory is not fully scanned.
Structured logs carry operational lineage and never contain secrets
Selected paths, including legacy fingerprint logs, are bounded; repository-wide log and Debug enforcement is absent.
Metrics cover bounded operational state and high-cardinality IDs are not permanent labels
Tunnel metrics are bounded; no repository-wide metric schema gate exists.
Liveness, readiness, dependencies, fleet, device and lease health are separated; liveness does not depend on a phone
Queues, maps and task spawning are bounded
Command and tunnel pending queues are bounded; no repository-wide scanner exists.
Protobuf field numbers are never reused, removed fields are reserved, and buf lint and breaking are mandatory
Canonical protobuf consumer API is introduced.
Database changes use expand-migrate-contract and rollback remains possible after expansion
Every process publishes release ID, Git SHA, protocol range, schema version and capabilities
Rollout follows package, integrity, signature, staging, canary, fleet rollout and post-deploy verification
Package integrity exists; signing, canary and fleet verification remain.
Permanent CI runs architecture and Python regressions, formatting, strict Clippy and workspace tests
Permanent CI eventually includes audit, deny, machete, semver, buf, gitleaks, migration and Android gates
Only the currently implemented permanent checks are claimed.
A completed production slice has invariant, use case, typed port, adapter, composition, auth, idempotency, audit, typed errors, metrics, tests, docs and compatibility proof
Domain code without production composition is not complete
